org.apache.flink.runtime.jobgraph

## Class JobGraph

  * [java.lang.Object](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")
  *     * org.apache.flink.runtime.jobgraph.JobGraph

  * All Implemented Interfaces:
    [Serializable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/io/Serializable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.io")

* * *




        public class JobGraph
        extends [Object](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")
        implements [Serializable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/io/Serializable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.io")

The JobGraph represents a Flink dataflow program, at the low level that the JobManager accepts. All programs from higher level APIs are transformed into JobGraphs.

The JobGraph is a graph of vertices and intermediate results that are connected together to form a DAG. Note that iterations (feedback edges) are currently not encoded inside the JobGraph but inside certain special vertices that establish the feedback channel amongst themselves.

The JobGraph defines the job-wide configuration settings, while each vertex and intermediate result define the characteristics of the concrete operation and intermediate data.

See Also:
    [Serialized Form](<../../../../../serialized-form.html#org.apache.flink.runtime.jobgraph.JobGraph>)

  *     * ### Constructor Detail

      * #### JobGraph

            public JobGraph([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") jobName)

Constructs a new job graph with the given name, the given [`ExecutionConfig`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/ExecutionConfig.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common"), and a random job ID. The ExecutionConfig will be serialized and can't be modified afterwards.

Parameters:
    `jobName` \- The name of the job.
      * #### JobGraph

            public JobGraph([@Nullable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/javax/annotation/Nullable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in javax.annotation")
                            [JobID](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/JobID.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common") jobId,
                            [String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") jobName)

Constructs a new job graph with the given job ID (or a random ID, if `null` is passed), the given name and the given execution configuration (see [`ExecutionConfig`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/ExecutionConfig.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common")). The ExecutionConfig will be serialized and can't be modified afterwards.

Parameters:
    `jobId` \- The id of the job. A random ID is generated, if `null` is passed.
    `jobName` \- The name of the job.
      * #### JobGraph

            public JobGraph([@Nullable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/javax/annotation/Nullable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in javax.annotation")
                            [JobID](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/JobID.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common") jobId,
                            [String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") jobName,
                            [JobVertex](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/jobgraph/JobVertex.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.jobgraph")... vertices)

Constructs a new job graph with the given name, the given [`ExecutionConfig`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/ExecutionConfig.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common"), the given jobId or a random one if null supplied, and the given job vertices. The ExecutionConfig will be serialized and can't be modified afterwards.

Parameters:
    `jobId` \- The id of the job. A random ID is generated, if `null` is passed.
    `jobName` \- The name of the job.
    `vertices` \- The vertices to add to the graph.

      * #### setExecutionConfig

            public void setExecutionConfig([ExecutionConfig](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/ExecutionConfig.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common") executionConfig)
                                    throws [IOException](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/io/IOException.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.io")

Sets the execution config. This method eagerly serialized the ExecutionConfig for future RPC transport. Further modification of the referenced ExecutionConfig object will not affect this serialized copy.

Parameters:
    `executionConfig` \- The ExecutionConfig to be serialized.
Throws:
    `[IOException](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/io/IOException.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.io")` \- Thrown if the serialization of the ExecutionConfig fails
      * #### addVertex

            public void addVertex([JobVertex](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/jobgraph/JobVertex.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.jobgraph") vertex)

Adds a new task vertex to the job graph if it is not already included.

Parameters:
    `vertex` \- the new task vertex to be added

      * #### addJar

            public void addJar([Path](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/core/fs/Path.html> "class in org.apache.flink.core.fs") jar)

Adds the path of a JAR file required to run the job on a task manager.

Parameters:
    `jar` \- path of the JAR file required to run the job on a task manager
      * #### addJars

            public void addJars([List](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/util/List.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.util")<[URL](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/net/URL.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.net")> jarFilesToAttach)

Adds the given jar files to the [`JobGraph`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/jobgraph/JobGraph.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.jobgraph") via [`addJar(org.apache.flink.core.fs.Path)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/jobgraph/JobGraph.html#addJar-org.apache.flink.core.fs.Path->).

Parameters:
    `jarFilesToAttach` \- a list of the [`URLs`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/net/URL.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.net") of the jar files to attach to the jobgraph.
Throws:
    `[RuntimeException](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/RuntimeException.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")` \- if a jar URL is not valid.
